title: Lisa The Vegetarian

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of episode with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but routine use

jokes.text: Just leave me in the car with the window open a crack. - That's the plan.
jokes.setup: Grampa suggests being left in the car like a dog
jokes.punchline: Homer matter-of-factly confirms that's exactly what they planned
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly neglect delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: from eight to God only knows
jokes.setup: Marge trying to say the park is fun for ages 'eight to eighty'
jokes.punchline: Trails off realizing how old that sounds
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about age ranges

jokes.text: What a load of crappy crap crap!
jokes.punchline: Blunt, crude dismissal of wholesome content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - crude but not gratuitously offensive

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry. But it was a 150 degrees in the car.
jokes.setup: Goldilocks scenario - someone sleeping in bed
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical excuse for breaking into fairy tale house
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of fairy tale logic with Homer's practical concerns

jokes.text: You're supposed to feed them pellets from the machine over there.
jokes.setup: Context suggests someone trying to feed animals inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction about proper feeding
jokes.explanation: Setup for visual gag - the humor is in what Homer was doing wrong

jokes.text: Allemande left and do-si-diddley-diddley-do
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic 'diddley' speech pattern incorporated into dance calls
jokes.explanation: Perfect integration of Ned's verbal tics into square dancing

jokes.text: Shut up! - Okilly-dokilly.
jokes.punchline: Ned's cheerful, characteristic response to hostility
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Ned's pathological niceness vs Homer's rudeness

jokes.text: Buenos ding-dong-diddly-días, senor.
jokes.punchline: Spanish Flanders incorporates Ned's speech pattern into Spanish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - the Flanders speech pattern is genetic/cultural

jokes.text: Eh, a-googilly-doogilly.
jokes.punchline: Even upper-class Flanders has the verbal tic
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the universal Flanders trait gag

jokes.text: I know! I'll throw my own barbecue! The greatest barbecue this town has ever seen. And I'll only invite who I want. That'll show you.
jokes.setup: Homer feeling excluded from Flanders family event
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty revenge plan delivered with childish logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer pettiness and poor reasoning

jokes.text: Can I come? - Sure. D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Immediately invites his 'enemy' then realizes the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - can't help being nice despite his petty revenge plan

jokes.text: You know what you should serve, Marge? More of these lamb chops. These are the best ever.
jokes.punchline: Ironic setup for Lisa's vegetarian crisis
jokes.explanation: Plot setup that becomes ironic in context of episode theme

jokes.text: You might say the extra ingredient is salt.
jokes.punchline: Marge's modest, practical 'secret ingredient'
jokes.explanation: Marge's characteristic modesty and practical cooking approach

jokes.text: Lisa, get ahold of yourself. This is 'lamb,' not 'a lamb.'
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic that grammar changes the moral reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - linguistic distinction somehow matters morally

jokes.text: What's the difference between this lamb and the one that kissed me? - This one spent two hours in the broiler.
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact answer about cooking time
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Homer's characteristic obliviousness to sentiment

jokes.text: Dad! Those all come from the same animal!
jokes.setup: Homer listing bacon, ham, pork chops as alternatives
jokes.punchline: Lisa's exasperated correction
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Homer's next line, Lisa as straight man

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, right, Lisa. A wonderful, magical animal.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out all pork products come from pig
jokes.punchline: Homer's dreamy, reverential description of pig
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons quote - Homer's bizarre reverence for pigs as food source becomes iconic

jokes.text: I think Lisa's right, Dad. Eating meat is ba-a-a-ad.
jokes.punchline: Bart bleats like a sheep while agreeing with Lisa
jokes.explanation: Bart making fun while seeming supportive - sheep sound for 'bad'

jokes.text: Compared to them, the public schools are a haven of enlightenment.
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing her family to school
jokes.punchline: Calling public schools 'enlightened' sets up the next scene's irony
jokes.explanation: Setup for ironic contrast with what happens at school

jokes.text: First, pin them down so they don't fly up and hit you in the eye.
jokes.punchline: Treating dead worms like they might attack students
jokes.explanation: Absurd teacher instruction treating dead animals as dangerous

jokes.text: My worm went in my mouth and then I ate it. Can I have a new one?
jokes.punchline: Ralph casually mentions eating his specimen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - innocent delivery of disturbing content

jokes.text: Oh, boy! Sleep! That's where I'm a Viking.
jokes.setup: Teacher tells Ralph to sleep while others learn
jokes.punchline: Ralph's bizarre confidence about his sleeping abilities
jokes.explanation: Iconic Ralph quote - perfectly captures his innocent absurdist logic

jokes.text: Possibly the meat loaf.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for food without meat
jokes.punchline: Suggesting meat loaf as vegetarian option
jokes.explanation: School lunch lady's oblivious response about meat products

jokes.text: Yum. It's rich in bunly goodness.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'bunly' to describe bread nutrition
jokes.explanation: Silly made-up nutrition term from institutional food service

jokes.text: Do you remember when you lost your passion for this work?
jokes.punchline: Existential question about career satisfaction in thankless job
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly philosophical moment from background character

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Two independent thought alarms in one day. The students are over-stimulated.
jokes.punchline: School has alarm system for independent thinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dystopian satire of educational conformity

jokes.text: I warned ya! Didn't I warn ya? That colored chalk was forged by Lucifer himself!
jokes.punchline: Willie's over-the-top religious paranoia about art supplies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character moment - Scottish superstition about mundane objects

jokes.text: Cartoons don't have messages, Lisa. They're just a bunch of hilarious stuff, you know, like people getting hurt and stuff.
jokes.punchline: Bart dismissing media analysis while describing exactly what Lisa criticized
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about animated violence while dismissing deeper meaning

jokes.text: Come to Homer's BBBQ. The extra 'B' is for BYOBB.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical acronym with extra letter
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic failure with invitation design

jokes.text: What's that extra 'B' for? - That's a typo.
jokes.punchline: Homer admits it's just a mistake, not intentional
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest admission deflates his attempt at cleverness

jokes.text: If I went to a barbecue and there was no meat, I would say, 'Yo, goober! Where's the meat?'
jokes.punchline: Specific scenario with 'goober' insult
jokes.explanation: Homer's specific social scenario planning

jokes.text: You don't win friends with salad.
jokes.setup: Homer's philosophy about meat vs vegetables
jokes.punchline: Becomes a musical chant with family
jokes.explanation: Became iconic catchphrase - simple, memorable, and perfectly Homer

jokes.text: For privacy's sake, let's call her Lisa S. No, that's too obvious. Uh, let's say L. Simpson
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to anonymize Lisa's identity
jokes.punchline: Second attempt is equally obvious
jokes.explanation: Classic bureaucratic incompetence from Skinner

jokes.text: You may remember me from such educational films as Two Minus Three Equals Negative Fun.! And Firecrackers: The Silent Killer.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous educational film titles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy McClure moment - absurd B-movie titles delivered with gravitas

jokes.text: Oh, hello, Bobby. - Jimmy.
jokes.setup: Troy addressing the student in the film
jokes.punchline: Gets his name wrong immediately
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Troy's insincerity and disconnection

jokes.text: Come on, Jimmy. Let's take a peek at the killing floor. Don't let the name throw you, Jimmy. It's not really a floor. It's more of a steel grating that allows material to sluice through so it can be collected and exported.
jokes.punchline: Description makes it sound much worse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - attempt at euphemism makes industrial slaughter more disturbing

jokes.text: Just ask this scientician.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'scientician'
jokes.explanation: Great made-up word that sounds authoritative but meaningless

jokes.text: Don't kid yourself, Jimmy. If a cow ever got the chance, he'd eat you and everyone you care about.
jokes.punchline: Portraying cows as malevolent predators
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic turning herbivores into threats - became classic quote

jokes.text: Yes, you were, Jimmy. Yes, you were.
jokes.setup: Jimmy calling himself a moron for questioning meat
jokes.punchline: Troy's condescending agreement
jokes.explanation: Troy's condescending delivery caps the propaganda sequence

jokes.text: They can't seriously expect us to swallow that tripe. Now as a special treat, courtesy of our friends at the meat council... please help yourselves to this tripe.
jokes.punchline: Teacher immediately offers literal tripe (organ meat)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - metaphorical 'tripe' immediately followed by literal tripe

jokes.text: When I grow up, I'm going to Bovine University.
jokes.punchline: Child genuinely inspired by obvious corporate manipulation
jokes.explanation: Callback to propaganda film showing its effectiveness on children

jokes.text: It's tomato soup served ice cold.
jokes.punchline: Marge's unappetizing but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Marge's practical but unappetizing food description

jokes.text: Go back to Russia.
jokes.punchline: Cold War era xenophobic response
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical American response to unfamiliar food

jokes.text: I've got the prescription for you, Doctor. Another hot beef injection.
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude medical metaphor for serving meat
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate medical innuendo - mildly crude

jokes.text: I can't quite seem to... stand up under my own power anymore. - One whopper for the copper.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum has eaten too much
jokes.punchline: Homer's rhyme while serving more food to immobilized guest
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Wiggum's gluttony and Homer's cheerful rhyming

jokes.text: The pig de résistance.
jokes.setup: Homer presenting the whole roasted pig
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'pièce de résistance'
jokes.explanation: Homer's malapropism - 'pig' instead of 'pièce' is appropriately obvious

jokes.text: It's just a little dirty. It's still good. It's still good.
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate rationalization
jokes.explanation: First stage of Homer's denial about ruined pig

jokes.text: It's just a little slimy. It's still good. It's still good.
jokes.punchline: Homer maintaining denial as situation worsens
jokes.explanation: Second stage of escalating denial sequence

jokes.text: It's just a little airborne. It's still good. It's still good. - It's gone. - I know.
jokes.setup: Pig now flying through the air
jokes.punchline: Homer's final absurd rationalization, then defeat
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of escalating denial - 'airborne' is hilariously inadequate

jokes.text: You know, Smithers, I think I'll donate a million dollars to the local orphanage... when pigs fly. - That's a good one. Hey- Hey-
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns using 'when pigs fly' expression
jokes.punchline: Pig literally flies past window at that moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy timing - literal interpretation of common expression

jokes.text: Will you be donating that million dollars now, sir? - No. I'd still prefer not.
jokes.setup: Smithers pointing out pig actually flew
jokes.punchline: Burns finds technicality to avoid charity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - finding loopholes to avoid generosity

jokes.text: You dunking your sausages in that syrup, homeboy?
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriate casual tone and crude description
jokes.explanation: Bart's crude interpretation of Homer's syrup request

jokes.text: Why don't you just eat him, Dad?
jokes.punchline: Bart suggesting Homer eat him
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Bart escalating the meat-eating argument

jokes.text: That's it! I can't live in a house with this prehistoric carnivore!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dramatic exit line calling Homer prehistoric
jokes.explanation: Lisa's overly dramatic response fits her character's intensity

jokes.text: Are you gonna marry a carrot, Lisa? - Yes, I'm gonna marry a carrot. - She admitted it! She admitted she's gonna marry a carrot!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic response taken literally
jokes.explanation: Playground logic - kids taking sarcasm literally

jokes.text: Are you gonna marry a carrot, Lisa? - Yes, I'm gonna marry a carrot. - She admitted it! - She admitted she's gonna marry a carrot!
jokes.setup: Kids tease Lisa about being vegetarian
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic response taken literally and spread
jokes.explanation: Kids being kids - taking sarcasm literally and spreading rumors

jokes.text: I take it from your yelling that you like my tofu dogs.
jokes.setup: Lisa screaming in despair about eating meat
jokes.punchline: Apu's misinterpretation of her emotional state
jokes.explanation: Apu's characteristically understated response to Lisa's crisis

jokes.text: But what do you do if somebody wants a nonalcoholic beer? - You know, it's never come up.
jokes.setup: Secret beer garden behind convenience store
jokes.punchline: Apu's honest admission about customer base
jokes.explanation: Observational humor about convenience store customer demographics

jokes.text: Back then I was known as the fifth Beatle. - Sure you were, Apu.
jokes.setup: Apu claiming to be fifth Beatle
jokes.punchline: Paul McCartney's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Absurdist moment - actual Beatle dismissing convenience store clerk's claim

jokes.text: You'd be surprised how often you'd find a big hunk of pork in them.
jokes.punchline: Unexpected meat contamination in vegetarian products
jokes.explanation: Observational humor about food industry issues

jokes.text: In fact, if you play 'Maybe I'm Amazed' backwards... you'll hear a recipe for a really ripping lentil soup.
jokes.setup: Reference to Beatles backwards masking rumors
jokes.punchline: Instead of satanic messages, it's a soup recipe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Beatles conspiracy theories with mundane vegetarian reality

jokes.text: Oh! Then you must think I'm a monster. - Yes, indeed, I do think that.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about Apu judging her for eating cheese
jokes.punchline: Apu's blunt honest agreement
jokes.explanation: Apu's characteristic blunt honesty followed by wisdom about tolerance

jokes.text: It was 'Live and Let Die.' - Whatever, whatever. It had a good rhythm.
jokes.punchline: Apu's dismissive response to correction from the actual Beatle
jokes.explanation: Apu's stubborn dismissal of correction from the song's actual creator

jokes.text: Rock stars. Is there anything they don't know?
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning advice from Paul and Linda McCartney
jokes.punchline: Homer's sincere belief in celebrity wisdom
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic faith in celebrity authority

jokes.text: I understand, honey. I used to believe in things when I was a kid.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating idealism as childish phase
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect Homer moment - cynical acceptance disguised as understanding

jokes.text: Come on! I'll give you a piggyback ride- Uh, oops! I mean, a veggieback ride home.
jokes.setup: Homer offering piggyback ride to vegetarian daughter
jokes.punchline: Catches himself and creates awkward vegetarian alternative
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment trying to be sensitive with clumsy wordplay

tmdb_overview: After visiting a local petting zoo, Lisa becomes a vegetarian and faces ridicule from family and friends. When she lashes out and ruins Homer's barbecue, she learns a lesson in tolerance.
insight: Vegetarian ethics debate carries 67 jokes at steady 3.62-per-minute pace, just shy of elite.
